questions.q: If a wave travels from one medium to another, then what will happen to the speed of the wave?
questions.answer: The speed of the wave will change.

questions.q: A string of length 40 cm is fixed at both ends. If it vibrates in its third harmonic, what is the distance between two consecutive nodes?
questions.answer: The distance between two consecutive nodes is 20 cm.

questions.q: A wave is described by the equation y = 0.2 sin (3x - 2t). Which type of wave is this?
A water wave of wavelength 2 m is observed to travel at a speed of 0.5 m/s. What is the frequency of the wave?
questions.answer: The frequency of the wave is 0.25 Hz.

questions.q: If two waves of equal frequency and amplitude travel in the same direction, then what will be the resultant amplitude?
questions.answer: The resultant amplitude will be zero.

questions.q: A tuning fork of frequency 256 Hz produces a sound wave of wavelength 1.25 m. What is the speed of the sound wave?
questions.answer: The speed of the sound wave is 320 m/s.

questions.q: In a stationary wave, the distance between two consecutive nodes is 15 cm. What is the wavelength of the wave?
questions.answer: The wavelength of the wave is 30 cm.

questions.q: A wave has a frequency of 50 Hz and an amplitude of 1.5 m. What is the maximum acceleration of the particles of the wave?
questions.answer: The maximum acceleration of the particles of the wave is 2250 m/s^2.

questions.q: A wave is described by the equation y = 2 cos (10t + x). What is the speed of the wave?
questions.answer: The speed of the wave is 1/10 m/s.

questions.q: A string of mass 20 g and length 50 cm is fixed at both ends. If it vibrates in its second harmonic, what is the tension in the string?
questions.answer: We know that for a string fixed at both ends, the speed of the wave is given by v = √(T/m) and the frequency of the second harmonic is f2 = 3v/2L. We are given the mass and length of the string, so we can calculate the speed of the wave and then the tension in the string.
